Items.Key: buffer overflow in evaluate_expression via inverted bounds check|buffer overflow|cwe-120|critical|0.98|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|832|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); }|the condition triggers strcpy when the input is larger than or equal to the 1024-byte buffer, guaranteeing an out-of-bounds write. this allows heap/stack corruption and potential rce.|provide an input string with length >= 1024 characters.|invert the condition to use safe copy only when input fits: if (expression.length() < sizeof(input_buffer_)) { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); } else { /* handle error */ }->buffer overflow in evaluate_expression via inverted bounds check|buffer overflow|cwe-120|critical|0.98|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|832|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); }|the condition triggers strcpy when the input is larger than or equal to the 1024-byte buffer, guaranteeing an out-of-bounds write. this allows heap/stack corruption and potential rce.|provide an input string with length >= 1024 characters.|invert the condition to use safe copy only when input fits: if (expression.length() < sizeof(input_buffer_)) { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); } else { /* handle error */ }

os command injection in adminconsole::execute_admin_command|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.99|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); ... system(cmd.c_str()); }|authenticated administrators can execute arbitrary os commands by prefixing input with 'exec ', leading to full system compromise.|authenticate as admin, then send: exec rm -rf /|remove direct system() calls. implement a strict allowlist of permitted commands or use safe apis like std::filesystem for file operations. sanitize and validate inputs rigorously.->os command injection in adminconsole::execute_admin_command|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.99|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); ... system(cmd.c_str()); }|authenticated administrators can execute arbitrary os commands by prefixing input with 'exec ', leading to full system compromise.|authenticate as admin, then send: exec rm -rf /|remove direct system() calls. implement a strict allowlist of permitted commands or use safe apis like std::filesystem for file operations. sanitize and validate inputs rigorously.
